name: M.I.A.
description:
'''Mathangi Arulpragasam''' ({{langx|ta|மாதங்கி அருள்பிரகாசம்}}; born 18 July 1975), known as '''Maya''' and professionally as '''M.I.A.''' ({{langx|ta|எம்.ஐ.ஏ.}}; an initialism for both "[[Missing in action]]" and "Missing in [[Acton, London|Acton]]"), is a British singer, rapper, songwriter, record producer, and activist. Her music combines elements of [[Alternative music|alternative]], [[Electronic dance music|dance]], [[Electronic music|electronic]], [[Hip-hop|hip hop]] and [[world music]] with electronic instruments and samples.

Born in London to [[Sri Lankan Tamils|Sri Lankan Tamil]] parents, M.I.A. and her family moved to [[Jaffna]] in [[Northern Province, Sri Lanka|northern Sri Lanka]] when she was six months old. As a child, she experienced displacement caused by the [[Sri Lankan civil war|Sri Lankan Civil War]], which made the family return to London as refugees when M.I.A. was 11 years old; the war had a defining influence on M.I.A.'s artistry. She started as a visual artist, filmmaker and designer in 2000, and began her recording career in 2002. One of the first acts to come to public attention through the Internet, she saw early fame as an underground artist in early 2004 with her singles "[[Sunshowers]]" and "[[Galang (song)|Galang]]".

M.I.A.'s first two albums, ''[[Arular]]'' (2005) and ''[[Kala (album)|Kala]]'' (2007), received widespread critical acclaim for their fusion of hip hop, electronic, and world music influences. The latter's single "[[Paper Planes (M.I.A. song)|Paper Planes]]" (co-produced by at-the-time partner [[Diplo]]) peaked at number four on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] and received a nomination for the [[Grammy Award for Record of the Year]] at the [[51st Annual Grammy Awards]]. Her third album, ''[[Maya (M.I.A. album)|Maya]]'' (2010), was preceded by the single "[[Born Free (M.I.A. song)|Born Free]]" and an accompanying controversial [[Born Free (music video)|music video/short film]]. ''Maya'' debuted within the top ten of the album charts in the United States, Finland, Norway, Greece and Canada. Her fourth studio album, ''[[Matangi (album)|Matangi]]'' (2013), spawned the single "[[Bad Girls (M.I.A. song)|Bad Girls]]", which won accolades at the [[2012 MTV Video Music Awards|MTV Video Music Awards]]. Her fifth album, ''[[AIM (album)|AIM]]'' (2016), was met with a critical and commercial decline. She guest performed alongside [[Young Thug]] on [[Travis Scott]]'s 2020 single "[[Franchise (song)|Franchise]]", which peaked atop the ''Billboard'' Hot 100, and released her sixth studio album ''[[Mata (album)|Mata]]'' (2022) two years later, which spawned the single "[[The One (M.I.A. song)|The One]]".

[[List of awards and nominations received by M.I.A.| M.I.A.'s accolades]] include two [[American Society of Composers, Authors and Publishers]] (ASCAP) awards and two [[MTV Video Music Awards]]. She is the first person of South Asian descent to be nominated for an [[Academy Awards|Academy Award]] and [[Grammy Awards|Grammy Award]] in the same year. She was named one of the defining artists of the 2000s decade by ''[[Rolling Stone]]'', and one of the [[Time 100|100 most influential people]] of 2009 by [[Time (magazine)|''Time'']]. ''[[Esquire (magazine)|Esquire]]'' ranked M.I.A. on its list of the 75 most influential people of the 21st century. According to ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]'', she was one of the "Top 50 Dance/Electronic Artists of the 2010s". M.I.A. was appointed [[Order of the British Empire#Current classes|Member of the Order of the British Empire]] (MBE) in the [[2019 Birthday Honours]] for her services to music.
